name: sim_spectral_triple_weyl_hopf_coupling
claim: D-spectrum splits into L/R eigenspaces under Cl(3) chirality grading AND reversed Hopf∘Weyl ordering shifts spectrum vs Weyl∘Hopf (non-commutativity witnessed by sympy charpoly inequality).

pytorch.reason: numpy sufficient for finite-dim spectral computation here

pyg.reason: no graph message-passing needed; this is a spectral algebra test

z3.reason: sympy charpoly comparison is the decisive tool; z3 not needed

cvc5.reason: not needed; sympy handles symbolic polynomial inequality

sympy.reason: Constructs symbolic characteristic polynomials from eigenvalue sets and compares them to prove the two operator orderings (Weyl∘Hopf vs Hopf∘Weyl) produce distinct D-spectra; charpoly inequality is the load-bearing claim, not a numeric tolerance check.

clifford.reason: Cl(3) pseudoscalar I_3=e1*e2*e3 realizes the Weyl chirality grading (gamma5 analogue); confirms I_3^2=-1 (anti-involution) and anti-commutation with grade-1 vectors, which grounds the L/R eigenspace split in geometric algebra rather than ad-hoc labels.

geomstats.reason: Hopf fibration realized algebraically via Cl(3); geomstats redundant

e3nn.reason: SO(3) equivariance not the claim here; Weyl chirality is Cl(3)-native

rustworkx.reason: no graph structure required for spectral triple

toponetx.reason: cell complex topology not load-bearing here

gudhi.reason: persistent homology not relevant to spectral splitting
